lesson.title: Solving Quadratics by Square Roots
lesson.body: When a quadratic has no middle term (b = 0), use the **square root method**: (1) Isolate x¬≤. (2) Take ¬Ī‚ąö of both sides. Remember ¬Ī ‚ÄĒ there are two roots. Example: 3x¬≤ ‚ąí 48 = 0 ‚Üí 3x¬≤ = 48 ‚Üí x¬≤ = 16 ‚Üí x = ¬Ī4. This also works when the squared expression is a binomial: (x ‚ąí 3)¬≤ = 25 ‚Üí x ‚ąí 3 = ¬Ī5 ‚Üí x = 8 or x = ‚ąí2. Works best when the equation is already in the form (ax + b)¬≤ = c.

rooms.question: Solve: x¬≤ = 49
rooms.explanation: x¬≤=49 ‚Üí x=¬Ī‚ąö49=¬Ī7. Both 7 and ‚ąí7 are solutions because 7¬≤=49 and (‚ąí7)¬≤=49. Forgetting the ¬Ī is the most common error ‚ÄĒ x=7 alone is incomplete.

rooms.question: Solve: 2x¬≤ ‚ąí 50 = 0
rooms.explanation: Isolate x¬≤: 2x¬≤=50 ‚Üí x¬≤=25 ‚Üí x=¬Ī5. Note: ¬Ī‚ąö25 = ¬Ī5 (same answer) ‚ÄĒ option A is technically equivalent but not simplified. Standard form is ¬Ī5.

rooms.question: Solve: (x + 3)¬≤ = 16
rooms.explanation: Take ¬Ī‚ąö: x+3=¬Ī4. Case 1: x+3=4 ‚Üí x=1. Case 2: x+3=‚ąí4 ‚Üí x=‚ąí7. Two solutions: x=1 or x=‚ąí7. Check: (1+3)¬≤=16 ‚úď and (‚ąí7+3)¬≤=(‚ąí4)¬≤=16 ‚úď.

rooms.question: Solve: 3(x ‚ąí 1)¬≤ = 75
rooms.explanation: Divide by 3: (x‚ąí1)¬≤=25. Take ¬Ī‚ąö: x‚ąí1=¬Ī5. Case 1: x‚ąí1=5 ‚Üí x=6. Case 2: x‚ąí1=‚ąí5 ‚Üí x=‚ąí4. Check: 3(5)¬≤=75 ‚úď and 3(‚ąí5)¬≤=75 ‚úď.

rooms.question: Order the steps to solve: 4(x + 2)¬≤ ‚ąí 36 = 0
rooms.explanation: Isolate the squared expression ‚Üí divide ‚Üí take ¬Ī‚ąö ‚Üí solve the two linear equations. Check: 4(3)¬≤‚ąí36=36‚ąí36=0 ‚úď and 4(‚ąí3)¬≤‚ąí36=36‚ąí36=0 ‚úď.

questions.question: Solve: 5x¬≤ + 20 = 0
questions.explanation: 5x¬≤=‚ąí20 ‚Üí x¬≤=‚ąí4. A square cannot be negative in the real numbers, so there is no real solution. The solutions are x=¬Ī2i (complex), but those are outside this course's scope.

questions.question: Solve: (2x ‚ąí 1)¬≤ = 9
questions.explanation: Take ¬Ī‚ąö: 2x‚ąí1=¬Ī3. Case 1: 2x‚ąí1=3 ‚Üí 2x=4 ‚Üí x=2. Case 2: 2x‚ąí1=‚ąí3 ‚Üí 2x=‚ąí2 ‚Üí x=‚ąí1. Solutions: x=2 or x=‚ąí1. Check: (4‚ąí1)¬≤=9 ‚úď and (‚ąí2‚ąí1)¬≤=9 ‚úď.

questions.question: A ball dropped from height h falls s = 5t¬≤ meters in t seconds. From a cliff 80 m high, when does it land?
questions.explanation: 5t¬≤=80 ‚Üí t¬≤=16 ‚Üí t=¬Ī4. Since time is positive, t=4 seconds. The ¬Ī4 answer is mathematically correct but context eliminates the negative: we can't have t=‚ąí4. Always check physical meaning when solving real-world problems.
